You ask me there are a couple categories of games that “deserve remakes”
A) games that had a lot of potential with their idea, but were either poorly done or were held back by the tech of the time
B) games that are difficult or impossible to obtain today and cannot be ported

Thoughts?

data.body: Gramsci's core insight: in the West, power is maintained through hegemony — consent manufactured in civil society (media, schools, culture) — not just coercion. Challenging power requires a 'war of position': slow, patient work winning hearts in civil society before you can win the state.

data.body: Second question: is AI a hyperobject? Morton's definition: entities so distributed in time and space they defeat traditional ideas about what a thing is. Five properties: viscous, nonlocal, phased, interobjective, molten.
